╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║                   🎉 YOUR GITHUB ISSUES ARE READY! 🎉                        ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ⚠️  IMPORTANT: You Have THREE Options for Creating Issues                   │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─ Option 1: GitHub Copilot Workbench (Natural Language → Issues) ────────────┐
│ ✨ BEST FOR: Creating issues from scratch with natural language             │
│                                                                              │
│ How it works:                                                                │
│   1. Go to https://github.com/copilot or your repository                    │
│   2. Describe your feature: "@copilot create an epic for..."               │
│   3. Copilot generates parent + sub-issues                                   │
│   4. Review and create - done!                                              │
│                                                                              │
│ ✅ No sandbox interference                                                   │
│ ✅ Direct GitHub integration                                                 │
│ ✅ Understands natural language                                              │
│                                                                              │
│ 📖 Full guide: .github/COPILOT_WORKBENCH_GUIDE.md                           │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─ Option 2: Automation Script (Pre-Written Templates → Issues) ──────────────┐
│ ✨ BEST FOR: Batch creating the pre-written EPIC specifications             │
│                                                                              │
│ How it works:                                                                │
│   1. Review ISSUE_EPIC_1.md and ISSUE_EPIC_2.md                            │
│   2. Run: ./.github/create-epic-issues.sh                                  │
│   3. Done - 15 issues created in 30 seconds!                                │
│                                                                              │
│ ✅ Creates 2 epics + 13 sub-issues from templates                           │
│ ✅ Fast batch creation                                                       │
│ ❌ Not for natural language (uses templates)                                │
│                                                                              │
│ 📖 Full guide: .github/START_HERE.md                                        │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─ Option 3: Manual Creation (GitHub UI) ─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ ✨ BEST FOR: Simple issues or full manual control                           │
│                                                                              │
│ How it works:                                                                │
│   1. Go to repository Issues tab                                            │
│   2. Click "New Issue"                                                       │
│   3. Fill in details manually                                               │
│   4. Submit                                                                  │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║ 🤔 Confused About Sandboxes?                                                 ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

Read: .github/SANDBOX_VS_WORKBENCH_EXPLAINED.md

KEY POINTS:
✅ The sandbox is for AI coding agents, not you
✅ Copilot Workbench is NOT sandboxed - you use it directly
✅ You CAN create issues from natural language without any barriers
✅ The automation script is optional - only for batch creating templates

┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ 📚 START HERE: Read .github/START_HERE.md                                    │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║ 🚀 ONE COMMAND TO CREATE ALL ISSUES                                          ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

    ./.github/create-epic-issues.sh

    ✅ Creates 15 issues in ~30 seconds
    ✅ 2 parent epics + 13 sub-issues
    ✅ All properly linked and labeled
    ✅ Ready for GitHub Copilot integration

╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║ 📦 WHAT YOU GET                                                              ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

┌─ Epic #1: Time-Based Order Fulfillment ─────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Priority: HIGH | Story Points: 16 | Estimated: 8-10 days                    │
│                                                                              │
│ Sub-Issues:                                                                  │
│   🔹 1.1: Order Validator Service (3 SP)                                    │
│   🔹 1.2: Checkout Pane - Dynamic Form Alteration (5 SP)                    │
│   🔹 1.3: Order Placement Validation Event Subscriber (2 SP)                │
│   🔹 1.4: Admin Configuration Form (2 SP)                                   │
│   🔹 1.5: Automated Testing (3 SP)                                          │
│   🔹 1.6: Documentation & User Guide (1 SP)                                 │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

┌─ Epic #2: Delivery Radius Validation ───────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Priority: MEDIUM | Story Points: 15 | Estimated: 6-8 days                   │
│                                                                              │
│ Sub-Issues:                                                                  │
│   🔹 2.1: Enhance Delivery Radius Calculator Service (2 SP)                 │
│   🔹 2.2: Shipping Method Integration (2 SP)                                │
│   🔹 2.3: Interactive Map Component (Frontend) (4 SP)                       │
│   🔹 2.4: Checkout Pane - Address Validation UI (3 SP)                      │
│   🔹 2.5: Admin Configuration & Store Settings (1 SP)                       │
│   🔹 2.6: Automated Testing (2 SP)                                          │
│   🔹 2.7: Documentation & User Guide (1 SP)                                 │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘

╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║ 📚 DOCUMENTATION FILES                                                       ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

├─ 🟢 START_HERE.md              👈 READ THIS FIRST!
│  └─ Quick start guide, prerequisites, next steps
│
├─ 📖 HOW_TO_CREATE_ISSUES.md    (Complete user guide)
│  └─ Detailed instructions, troubleshooting, Copilot tips
│
├─ 📄 GITHUB_ISSUES_README.md    (Quick reference)
│  └─ Overview, file structure, quick commands
│
├─ 👁️ VISUAL_EXAMPLE.md          (See how it looks)
│  └─ Visual mockups, progress tracking examples
│
├─ 📋 ISSUE_EPIC_1.md            (Epic specification)
│  └─ Full details for Epic #1
│
└─ 📋 ISSUE_EPIC_2.md            (Epic specification)
   └─ Full details for Epic #2

╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║ ⚡ PREREQUISITES (One-time setup)                                            ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

1. Install GitHub CLI:
   • macOS:         brew install gh
   • Ubuntu/Linux:  sudo apt install gh
   • Windows:       winget install GitHub.cli

2. Authenticate:
   gh auth login
   (Follow the prompts - takes 1 minute)

3. Done! Ready to create issues.

╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║ 🎯 AFTER CREATING ISSUES                                                     ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

✅ View issues:     gh issue list --label epic
✅ Create project:  Set up a GitHub Project board
✅ Assign tasks:    gh issue edit <number> --add-assignee <username>
✅ Start coding:    git checkout -b feature/epic1-sub1.1
✅ Use Copilot:     Reference issue numbers in your code

╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║ 🤖 GITHUB COPILOT INTEGRATION                                                ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

Once issues are created, you can:

In your IDE:
    // @copilot: Based on issue #124, implement the OrderValidator service

In Copilot Chat:
    @workspace Help me implement issue #124 from Epic #1

In Pull Requests:
    Closes #124
    
    This PR implements Sub-Issue 1.1: Order Validator Service
    - ✅ Created OrderValidator.php
    - ✅ All tests passing

GitHub will automatically:
    ✅ Link PR to the sub-issue
    ✅ Update parent epic's checklist when merged
    ✅ Show progress on the epic

╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║ 📊 SUMMARY                                                                   ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

Total Issues:       15 (2 epics + 13 sub-issues)
Total Story Points: 31 SP
Estimated Time:     14-18 days of development
Files Created:      8 files (75+ KB documentation)
Automation:         100% automated creation

╔══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╗
║ ✅ YOU'RE ALL SET!                                                           ║
╚══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════╝

To create your issues now, just run:

    ./.github/create-epic-issues.sh

The script will create all 15 issues and give you the issue numbers.
Then you can start assigning tasks and developing! 🚀

Questions? See START_HERE.md or HOW_TO_CREATE_ISSUES.md

Good luck with your project! 🎉
